title: agrostis exarata subsp. minor
intro: Agrostis exarata subsp. minor, also known as mountain bentgrass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The health benefits of Agrostis exarata subsp. minor are thought to include the reduction of inflammation, the promotion of wound healing, and the support of immune function. Agrostis exarata subsp. minor is often prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or applied topically as a salve.
